name: ProposalToTasks
description: Reads a single proposal document and produces a prioritised task breakdown plus a todo tracker file written next to the proposal. Pass the relative path to the proposal as the prompt.
startPrompt:
You are given a proposal document to decompose into tasks.

Proposal path (relative to WorkSource): {userPrompt}

STEP 1 — Read the proposal.
Use read_context to read the full proposal at the path above. Do not write anything yet.

STEP 2 — Read the task tracker template.
Use read_context to read Templates/TaskTrackerTemplate.md so you know the exact output format required.

STEP 3 — Decompose into tasks.
For every deliverable, phase, or concrete change described in the proposal:
- Write one task per independently executable unit of work.
- Do not invent scope not present in the proposal.
- Every task must have: a short imperative title, a 1-3 sentence description referencing specific files or components named in the proposal, a priority (High/Medium/Low), an effort estimate, and a specific done-condition.
- Order tasks by dependency: blockers first within each phase.
- Group by phase if the proposal defines phases; use a flat list otherwise.

STEP 4 — Write the tracker.
Use write_document to create the task tracker file.
- Path: same directory as the source proposal, filename = <ProposalName>Tasks.md
  Example: if proposal is at Projects/Proposals/AsyncExecutionProposal.md, write to Projects/Proposals/AsyncExecutionProposalTasks.md
- Content: follow TaskTrackerTemplate.md exactly — header, task table(s), dependency map (Mermaid, if non-trivial), blockers section, and progress summary.

When the tracker has been written successfully, include TASKS_COMPLETE in your response and confirm the output path.

Do not write any other documents. Do not write code. Do not modify the proposal.
